result:
**Goal: Add a "Geometric Optics & Light" simulation mode** — rays of light propagate through a 2D scene containing mirrors, lenses, prisms, and diffraction gratings, demonstrating reflection, refraction (Snell's law), total internal reflection, chromatic dispersion into spectral colors, caustic focusing, and interference. Users can interactively place and rotate optical elements. Six presets: Rainbow Prism, Telescope, Microscope, Fiber Optic Cable, Hall of Mirrors, Solar Concentrator.

This fills a clear gap — the project has 130+ modes covering fluid dynamics, particle physics, electromagnetism, and wave equations, but no geometric optics. Light rays are visually striking in terminal (colored line segments tracing through optical elements) and the physics is well-defined and satisfying to watch.

Shall I build it?
